description_long: This is a vintage-style electric fan with a cylindrical motor housing and a hexagonal metal base, showing signs of wear and rust. The fan features four broad, slightly curved blades made of metal, painted in a faded teal color. A robust wire grille, composed of concentric rings and radial spokes, encloses the blades for safety, with a central hub at the front. The fan's upright support column is attached to the base and the motor housing, and the base itself has a metallic switch or button. The surface of the fan is predominantly matte with visible rust and patina, suggesting prolonged use in an urban or industrial environment. No visible power cord is present, and the control mechanism appears to be a single button on the base.
description: A weathered, teal metal electric fan with a wire grille, four blades, and a hexagonal base, showing rust and patina. The fan has a sturdy, industrial look with a single button on the base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cylindrical motor housing, upright support, and the wire grille protecting the blades.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the motor housing with ventilation holes and the hexagonal base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the support column and the side profile of the fan blades.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the four teal blades, the central hub, and the protective wire grille.
attribute_blade_guard_design: The blade guard is a wire grille made of concentric rings connected by radial spokes, forming a protective cage around the blades. It is metallic and slightly rusted.
